summary: The earthquake that struck near Mandalay in central Myanmar (magnitude 7.7) has been raised to the highest alert level: impact models now point to a major disaster, with heavy casualties possible across Mandalay and Sagaing, and humanitarian reporting has begun to confirm collapsed buildings and deaths. A strong aftershock (magnitude 6.7) hit the same area minutes after the main shock and is tracked separately. In the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo, the flood situation has eased a step, though rivers remain high and thousands of people are still displaced. Two further earthquakes overnight, near Vanuatu and in the mid-Atlantic, were strong but remote and are not expected to have caused harm. Myanmar is this morning's story: the estimates are grave, and confirmed reports are only beginning to catch up with them.

change_notes.note: Raised from the middle to the highest alert level overnight as impact models caught up with the earthquake's size and shallowness; ground reports of collapsed buildings are now linked.
A large aftershock in the same area, minutes after the main shock — tracked as its own event because it shook the same damaged buildings again.
Stepped down one alert level as river levels ease, but the displacement reported earlier still stands.

editorial_greens.reason: Poás volcano in Costa Rica is actively erupting near visited and populated areas; below the alert bar, but worth a line for readers in the region.
